message:
fix: React rendering compliance in MemoryDump and Streamdown tokenizer

MemoryDump (matrix hex rain animation):
- Convert mutable `let columns` and `let drops` to useRef so the
  animation callback reads from stable references rather than stale
  closure captures. This fixes the React 19 lint rule against writing
  to variables during render — the canvas draw loop now mutates
  ref.current instead of re-assigning local `let` bindings.
- The resize handler now correctly compares against columns.current
  and calls initDrops() which updates refs in place.

Streamdown inline tokenizer:
- Add React `key` props (using match.index) to all JSX nodes produced
  in tokenizeInline() — <code>, <a>, <strong>, <em> — to suppress
  React "missing key" warnings when the tokenizer outputs arrays.
- Simplify redundant `.endsWith()` guards: the regex already guarantees
  matched tokens are properly delimited, so checking both start and
  end is unnecessary. Checking the prefix alone is sufficient and
  easier to read.
- Add explanatory comments above the inline tokenizer regex describing
  the five token types it matches.
- Remove unnecessary nullish coalescing (`?? ""`) on match[0] since
  RegExp.exec() always returns a string for the full match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
